content:
Hello everyone, I'm 鯊魚腳下的野貓 aka Ernest the Silver hunter.
This time, I'd like to share with you my experience of using five **Aequor** teams to achieve 350↑ in the **D-Tide Zone**. I'll show you that Aequor players don't need to sacrifice their precious Silver to draw other realms' characters, and that they can also achieve a formation policy that allows them to get rewards of 350 every issue.

**※Note※** This article is for players who__ have obtained all the Aequor and Chaos characters__. You can only accept __a maximum of FOUR missing characters__.
If you are: a mid-game player, an experienced player with only a few Aequor characters, or a newbie just entering the game and watching from the sidelines, this article can be used as an introduction to the Aequor team with the approach of the D-Tide restricted area. You can:
- look ahead to the possibilities of the Aequor;
- become familiar with the matching concepts of the Aequor;
- get a couple of inspirations from the Aequor team to help you pass the level.
But __don't__: jump into the Aequor at the first sign of disagreement.

Reference picture: https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1266165440443449354

Shortcuts:
[Shield Strike Team](https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1308730391825354845/1308730454257565737)
[Tentacle Team](https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1308730391825354845/1308730508963745792)
[Retaliate Team](https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1308730391825354845/1308730546712612946)
[Goliath Team](https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1308730391825354845/1308730577070985266)
[Poison Team](https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1308730391825354845/1308730604095016992)
thread_title: The light of the abyss, dark blue shin in dreams︱D-Tide All-Aequor Workthrough

content:
# Team analysis
## Shield Strike <:proud:1239910976200114186> 
**Advantages**: Cheap (not dependent on relic, low awake requirements), stable (hard to be defeat), high lower limit (silentl sea makes damage easy to come up), versatile (no obvious restraint)
**Disadvantages**: Long-term fatigue (damage lacks growth, and the Ex would get harder to trigger when the times you use it)
**Strength at the normal fight**: ★★★★★
**Strength at the boss fight**: ★★★☆☆
**Main attribute**: Realm mastery (related to the amount of shielding in the silent sea)
**Posse Selection**: Short-Lived Eternity, Injection Guard, Embrace of the Bone-Chilling, All of Her

This is a relatively simple team to get started with. As long as the boss does not grow, or grows very slowly, it is very suitable for grinding through with the Shield Strike team. Usually the first and second waves of monster boss will tend towards this type.
Aequor Shield Strike cannot accumulate shields in the same way as Caro and Ultra to achieve explosive accumulation. It is more inclined to just click on silent sea and build a few shields to deal a not-low amount of damage. Therefore, Sanga's level requirement is higher than Alva's (although it is not impossible to train Alva mainly, it is just that I have given her very few resources, which has formed this opinion).
For Awakening, you only need Nautila. If you have a good encounter, you can consider Awake Alva to get Alert. For relics, it doesn't have much of an impact. This saves sigils, and Aequor Shield Strike is especially suitable for hitting the first and second waves of poor maps. It is such reliable, and the only important team members are Sanga, Alva and Nautila, so it can be used to spend a **Seizure** character.
Although the main attribute is realm mastery, the **Core** talent of Sanga is related to death resistance, so *‘Polar Night and Break of Dawn’* comes with any death resistance wheel as standard. For the bond, you can consider *Whispers of Burial Grounds*, *Scarlet Tremor* or *Twisted Twins White*, but the best choice is the *Ring Chamber 36*, which main pick cri damage.
thread_title: The light of the abyss, dark blue shin in dreams︱D-Tide All-Aequor Workthrough

content:
## Tentacle <:sleep:1238448786540462130> <:Heartbeat:1236951458176565389> 
**Advantages**: Versatile (no obvious restraint), long-term advantages (strong growth potential)
**Disadvantages**: Low lower limit (landing battle disabilities), expensive (reliance on relics, high awake requirements)
**Strength at the normal fight**: ★★☆☆☆
**Strength at the boss fight**: ★★★★☆
**Main attribute**: Realm mastery (related to the number of tentacle assembly)
**Posse selection**: Little Wish, Eternal Funeral, Giant's Dominance, All of Her

Once shrouded the Aequor realm phantasmal dive rankings for previous months of the strong team, can ignore the powerful versatility of the relics, Chrono Pendulum and boss, allowing the Tentaclel team to be placed in any wave. The reason why it doesn't have five stars is because I'm not used to using the Tentaclel team and I don't have the En3 for Tulu. This is the score I can give subjectively. But compared to phantasmal dive, the biggest problem is that Ramona may have to be used elsewhere, and the lack of a double command makes the Tentaclel team a bit shaky.
Although Ramona is missing, the Tentaclel team in the D-Tide Zone has gained greater flexibility. It can be compatible with Aurita or ‘24’ as the second carrier, simply spend a **Seizure** character, or give Tulu the aliemus recharge attribute to carry himself. Depending on the strategy, the matching of the Covenantis also very diverse, but it is still recommended to leave room on the wheel of life for *‘Emerg’* and *‘Frenzy’*, and if necessary, *‘Hope’* should also be counted in to reduce the pressure during the middle.
thread_title: The light of the abyss, dark blue shin in dreams︱D-Tide All-Aequor Workthrough

content:
## Retaliate <:cry:1243502872021438475> 
**Advantages**: Stable (heal with every move), high upper limit (damage grows exponentially with the number of times the enemy attacks with Protective Scales)
**Disadvantages**: Go overboard (limited effect on boss with less than two permanent attacks)
**Strength at the normal fight**: ★★★★☆
**Strength at the boss fight**: ★★★★☆
**Main attribute**: None
**Posse selection**: Resolve of the Mountains, The Only Seed, Crossing the Door of Retrospection, First Encounter in Pure White, Injection Guard

Aequor Retaliate has the most effective means of accumulating Retaliate teams  in the entire game, but its survivability is slightly lower than that of Ultra Retaliate. Caecus's strikes are the most powerful retaliate-transforming damage dealers. Although they require special investment in wheels and covenants with the sigil drop attribute, he can easily deal a lot of damage. For this reason, you should also consider attaching the ‘mirage’ orison on his Strike and Shattered Halberd.
In order to make up for the lack of initiative, it is recommended to bring Pandia, who is responsible for harvesting mobs with a frenzy burst, and control the timing of boss awakening to second blood during the boss fight. For example Shadow ‘24’ and Pendia are bosses that awaken with multiple attack in the first round.
Since Caecus's retaliate is based on damage, cri rate and cri damage is the most effective way to increase the efficiency of stacking retaliate. For this reason, he is currently a member of the version of persecution, but his targeting is still strong. Other members can consume some of the Wheels and Covenant that fall from sigil drop or death resistance, or they can increase the fault tolerance rate by using the Posse system.
I would recommend using your own Goliath to wear support wheels to carry Caecus, and then borrowing the big guy's Goliath to form a Goliath team. But if there is no competent multi-attack boss in the current D-Tide Zone, you can also use Caecus as a **Assistant** for one of the Goliath teams, and let Pandia serve as the second carry to solve the Ex window period of normal fight Goliath without *‘Analysis of Death’*.
thread_title: The light of the abyss, dark blue shin in dreams︱D-Tide All-Aequor Workthrough

content:
## Goliath <:laugh:1243502404394160208> 
**Advantages**: Stable (damage growing quickly, shortens battle time), cheap (not dependent on relics), long-term advantages (strong growth potential)
**Disadvantages**: Go overboard (appears to be flagging with non-power type boss), luck factor (varies greatly depending on whether or not you make ‘mirage’ on the Might Makes Right)
**Strength at the normal fight**: ★★★★★
**Strength at the boss fight**: ★★★☆☆
**Main Attribute**: Silver key rechar
**Posse Selection**: Giant's Dominance, Roaring Blood and Sand

Goliath is also known as the God of normal fight. *‘Giant’s Blade’* paired with *‘Analysis of Death’* can always be used to quickly pass through normal fights with minimal combat damage, and is suitable for dealing with the big map of fourth and fifth waves. After breaking away from the formulaic operations of the Goliath-Caecus Team, the Goliath Team only needs one or two sup, so that he can single-carry alone. It is also a team that can spend a  Seizure character.
The main problem for the Goliath is concentrated in the boss fight. In the first few rounds, you must survive, stack cards, grow, and then accumulate enough Goliath's Aliemus once. Against a boss like Helot, Karen or Jenkin, which can accumulate high power, you can try not to stack cards and directly attack the boss with two turns of bursts to reduce it to the second stage. Other bosses with less power must be a bit more patient. Note: Although Leigh will give us strength, due to the mechanism, Goliath is basically just like fighting a cornered beast when fighting Leigh.
thread_title: The light of the abyss, dark blue shin in dreams︱D-Tide All-Aequor Workthrough

content:
## Poison <:angry:1243503354689359893> 
**Advantages**: Long-term advantages (strong growth potential), versatility (no obvious restrictions)
**Disadvantages**: Unstable (brittle), low lower limit (landing battle disabilities)
**Strength at the normal fight**: ★★★☆☆
**Strength at the boss fight**: ★★★★☆
**Main attribute**: Strong attack
**Posse selection**: Sea Festival, Resolve of the Mountains, Little Wish, The Only Seed

Aequor Poison is a Poison team that makes up for the lack of poison by relying on a high number of triggers. With Nymphaea and Miryam as standard, there are two poison triggers. Usually, ‘24’ is also used here, which makes three poison triggers. Occasionally, Faros must also be spent here, which should be considered... 0.25?
But three poison trigger roles mean three damage type characters, with very weak survivability, and they must rely on the Rite to heal. But using the Rite to heal means sacrificing the growth of *‘Supreme Sacrifice’* and the anti-D-Tide support of ‘The Chosen One’. It requires a bit of the player's control of the situation and the ability to predict it. You need to know how far the poison stack has grown before you can focus on triggering poison with an Ex and then start eating the Rite.
It should be noted that Miryam is the main poisoner in this team, since the means of poison she causes is by card that can be used multiple times in a turn, unlike ‘24’ which is an EX and requires at least two rounds to be used once in Manic Persona, but she doesn't have much power and tentacle damage growth. Nymphaea and ‘24’ have limited attack abilities. They are  tools to assist survival, wrap up, and Ex. Therefore it is not impossible to give them a survival-oriented choice on the Wheel.
Due to Miryam's En3 effect, she can increase her basic damage by 45% by taking three Sacrifice each turn, and the Strong attack related to her basic damage can be supported by other team members. And Miryam's talent makes her most suitable for improving the attribute of cri damage. Covenant recommends choosing *Steppen Wolf* to compensate for the damage-to-poison reduction caused by the D-Tide.

content: Hello i made a guide to help me and others find the best Enlighten

chaos seem to be mising 24 

her best enlighten is the one that improves her 3-cost, which effectively turns it into a net 1 aritm. cost in depression.

<:heart:1239910691218001961> I have updated the sheet for 24 and Sorel, thank you!
Dont hesitate to give more feedback.

I think Tawil can stop at E2
since she can get crit from relics, supports and wheels
also I see what you did there with Winkle 
truly our limited
Since this is a f2p guide, I think it is worth mentioning that tulu will likely be moved to standard banner so you could save resources.
Tulu will have abyssal rate so his status won’t be that different

this for now ig
S
Cord - Free 30% Embryo Fusion every turn with the least drawback
Da Baby (Baby) - Free 50% (100%) Embryo Fusion at start of the battle, which means free 1 baby at start
Claw - Crit Rate 50% with self fragile for 3 turns, same as Cord, not much drawback, probably is for defense core team
Agate (Red Gem) - 8 temp power pet hit up to 3 times, max stacks double the power, so maxed at 48 Temp Power, just for 3 hit. I just like power so it's good

A
Inpspiration stacker, gain 1 to 4 inspiration every after battle, not good if you take it just before final battle
Candle - Max arit +2 with least drawback too, strong but sometimes not useful
Clown - Crazily good if you have 1 cost strong card, and it has Strike attribute that you can combine with other relic
Wand - 20% extra final damage, that's it
Mirror - 15 Crit Rate, extra 5% per card played
Tenctacle - Great but tricky, need increase max tentacle user
Fish (?) - Lose strenght but heal every hit to enemy
(Clothes - ...never mind that's B, but still free vulne and weakness at start of battle)
2nd mirror - Inflict weak to get 43 shield, Inflict vulne to get 15 temp power, inflict both to get both effect twice
Hand - Lose hp each turn but gain massive temp power and vigilance
Mask - Strong with certain team, 35 aliemus but +1 strike and defense cost
Ink - Why did i put it there it should be in B, but increase HP is nice sometimes, and good for retaliate team
Matches - Copy any card, that's it
Leaf (?) - 10 Crit rate, after first crit + 20% CR, after third crit + 50 Crit damage

Honorable mention
Astrolabe of the Arcane (that blue thingy with golden exterior) - 15 CR, additional 30 when hp under 25%, good but the condition is too far
His Oracle - Great when you have 4 rouse (which you should have)
Sharp Tounge - 20 Black sigil discount for every relic
Tentacle cursed relic not in s is certainly a choice
i rarely play aequor nowadays and i think -2 max tentacle is sometimes scary 😭
